description: Thorium Reader is a free and open-source EPUB reading application designed with accessibility as a core feature. This report documents test results from the W3C EPUB 3.3 Test Suite for Thorium Reader version 2.2.0.
evaluationMethodology: This report maps test results from the W3C EPUB 3.3 Consolidated Implementation Results (https://w3c.github.io/epub-tests/epub33/results) for Thorium Reader 2.2.0. Test results for all 155+ tests are based on consolidated results where implementation variants with identical core engines are merged. A test passes if any variant reports success.
conformanceSummary: Thorium Reader Desktop 2.2.0 demonstrates strong conformance across most EPUB Reading System 3.3 requirements. The reading system excels at core content handling, document processing, and navigation. Some optional features like Media Overlays and advanced scripting scenarios are still in development.

strengths: Complete core media type support (GIF, JPEG, PNG, SVG, WebP)
Proper package document and manifest processing
limitations: Media Overlays support in development (multiple todo/fail results)
Some scripting features not yet fully implemented
Limited support for advanced CSS EPUB extensions

testingEnvironments: Tested with W3C EPUB 3.3 Test Suite publications

changelog.description: Comprehensive EPUB 3.3 test results mapping - 155+ test cases from W3C consolidated results

productInfo.name: Thorium Reader Desktop
productInfo.description: Thorium Reader is a free, open-source EPUB reading application available for Windows, macOS, and Linux with no ads and no private data leaks.
